segments.text: Good evening, and welcome to the Trove News Network. I'm Vale Mercer.
And I'm Dorian Cole. Here's what moved across the floor this session.

story.head: Lawmakers clear a long-stalled infrastructure package

segments.text: Our top story: lawmakers have cleared a long-stalled infrastructure package overnight, directing sustained public funds toward construction. Contractors say the certainty alone is enough to restart projects that had been on the shelf.

story.head: A data-center wave drives compute and the power to run it

segments.text: Meanwhile in technology, a fresh data-center wave is pulling demand for compute and the power to run it. Suppliers are signalling tight lead times into next quarter.

story.head: A prolonged heatwave leans the grid on backup generation

segments.text: Over in energy, a prolonged heatwave has utilities leaning on backup generation and bracing for sustained peak load. Fuel and equipment demand is climbing in the affected regions.

story.head: Record gavel: a rare piece shatters its auction estimate

segments.text: And at the top end, a record gavel tonight — a rare piece sold for several multiples of its estimate, reigniting chatter about the collectibles market.
That's the rundown for this edition. We'll be back at the next bell with a fresh read on the floor.
From all of us here at the desk — good night.
